items.title: Adelaide man 'not criminally responsible' for housemate's murder, court finds

children.content: An Adelaide court has found Kristopher Robert Steele fatally stabbed his housemate, but is "not criminally responsible due to mental incompetence".

gallery.altText: Police in a street at Cowandilla.

thumbnail.altText: Police in a street at Cowandilla.

items.title: SA greyhound trainer banned for dog's 'unnecessary suffering'

children.content: One of SA greyhound racing's most prominent identities has been disqualified after a 12-month inquiry by the sport's controlling body.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt stands behind a greyhound in blue winner's jacket

items.title: Nose landing gear failure could have caused fatal plane crash, ATSB says

children.content: A plane crash that killed two people and injured several others in Adelaide earlier this year could have been caused by a failure in the plane's nose landing gear, an ongoing investigation has found.

image.altText: Smoke coming from buildings behind a fence

items.title: Union passes 'no confidence' vote against SA's top cop and deputy

children.content: South Australia's police union passes a vote of no confidence in the leadership of the police commissioner and his deputy, despite the top cop saying the move was "not reflective" of the feedback he received from officers.

gallery.altText: composite of grant stevens in police uniform and wade burns in a business suit

thumbnail.altText: composite of grant stevens in police uniform and wade burns in a business suit

avatar.alt: A woman stands with her arms crossed, looking at the camera

items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on SA coast

children.content: Three dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

image.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: 'Shaman' and partner withheld magic mushrooms use before fashion icon's death, inquest hears

children.content: A coronial inquest is investigating the death of Adelaide fashion icon Carol Foord, who used magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment prior to her death in June 2024.

image.altText: A woman in a white shirt is photographed outdoors with soft sunlight and greenery in the background.

items.title: SA pushes for AI data centres as industry says it's not asking for subsidies

children.content: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.

image.altText: Masses on cords in a computer server.

items.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme

children.content: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

image.altText: A close up of a woman with long grey hair, looking off camera

items.title: Chihuahua Jet takes to the air as more pets allowed in cabin

children.content: An Australian airline is allowing small cats and dogs on a range of flights, with other passengers appearing unconcerned about sharing the plane with furry creatures.

gallery.altText: A woman holding a small dog in a carrier at an airport with a sign for Paw-riority boarding

thumbnail.altText: A woman holding a small dog in a carrier at an airport with a sign for Paw-riority boarding

items.title: Melrose Park roadworks bite business as Crafers wombat crossing questioned

children.content: The installation of a raised zebra crossing in Crafers is causing delays for commuters and reducing car parks, while an intersection upgrade in Melrose Park has left a minor arterial route completely blocked off.

image.altText: Detour sign on a suburban road

items.title: Residents worried about dramatic increase to Menindee Lakes water release

children.content: Residents living along the Darling River are worried the decision to double water releases from the Menindee Lakes will remove the system's drought reserve. But authorities say it will help rebuild populations of Murray cod downstream.

image.altText: A sun setting behind clouds at Lake Menindee in outback NSW.

items.title: Tony Modra's family 'extremely grateful' for support after crash

children.content: The family of AFL icon Tony Modra has released a statement thanking the community for the "overwhelming show of love" they have received since he was seriously injured in a crash last week.

image.altText: A man wearing a black shirt smiles.

items.title: UN inquiry accuses Israel of deliberately targeting and killing Palestinian children

children.content: The Israeli government is condemning the inquiry and rejecting its findings, labelling the United Nations report "deeply flawed" and full of "errors and distortions" designed to push a "politically driven anti-Israel narrative".

image.altText: A young child looking through a makeshift tent's window.

images.altText: Harry Kane sinks to his knees and puts his head in his hands
Harry Kane sits on the grass and looks up at the ref to complain

articles.title: England's hunt for attacking spark fruitless, as 'positivity' takes over

children.content: England say they need to stay positive after a dour goal-less draw against Ghana, positivity that is hopefully easier to find than shots on target were against the Black Stars' low block in Boston.

image.altText: Harry Kane holds his head

articles.title: Ronaldo roars back to life, with his moments off the ball the most telling

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo came into Portugal’s second World Cup group game under a firestorm of criticism. He answered with two goals, and important signs he is willing to be a team player.

image.altText: Cristiano Ronaldo smirks in the tunnel before the game

children.content: England is held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by a resolute Ghana after Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice in a 5-0 Portugal win over Uzbekistan. Follow live.

items.title: England frustrated by resolute Ghana, Croatia breaks through against Panama
'I get a lot of anxiety': Borrowers caught out with high-interest car loans

children.content: A new report warns customers about traps they face when taking out a car loan, including high-interest rates and fees.

items.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'

children.content: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

image.altText: Water bursts from two points in a long concrete flume.

items.title: The 'uncontrolled' police department behind fatal shooting of Perth girl

children.content: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting, as a senior Pakistani lawyer takes aim at the "uncontrolled" police department at the centre of her death.

items.title: Experts ask why triple murderer was granted bail before deadly spree

children.content: Sophie Quinn sought police protection from her former partner. Less than two months later he murdered her and two others during a shooting spree at Lake Cargelligo.

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: Five key takeaways from Crisafulli's second budget

children.content: This year's Queensland budget includes no "sugar hits" when it comes to cost of living, a big interest repayment bill and plenty of construction projects in the pipeline.

image.altText: Two men in suits standing at podiums with flags on either side.

items.title: Budget offers short-term sugar hit but there could be bitterness to come

children.content: Faced with rising interest rates, high grocery prices and bloated bills, the cost-of-living help announced in Tuesday's budget will only make a minor dent in household budgets, Nick Dole writes.

children.content: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

items.title: Cheap and easy ways to heat the human instead of the home

children.content: Heating your whole house — or even a room — can be expensive in the depths of winter. Keeping the humans inside it warm is often cheaper and more efficient.

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

items.title: Whistleblower doctors net $9.9m in NSW budget for robotic surgery

children.content: A long-running campaign to secure robotic surgery in western NSW has succeeded with the NSW budget allocating almost $10 million for equipment.

items.title: Australians financially supporting family in Lebanon say they have no other option

items.title: Injury mars Australia's crushing T20 World Cup win over Pakistan

children.content: Australia cruises to a comfortable 113-run victory over Pakistan at Headingley, but there may be concern over a finger injury to one of their stars.

items.title: Aussies produce mixed results in Wimbledon qualifying

items.title: Councillor vows to keep working despite suspension for misconduct

children.content: Ashleigh Vandenberg, a councillor in Melbourne's outer-west, plans to continue in her role, despite being handed a two-month suspension for misconduct.
